query_normalized: analyzing the trade‑off between prompt size and code generation quality computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on text-to-image synthesis and the specific challenge of "counterfactual size" in visual generation, which is an off-domain application compared to the user's question about code generation quality. While both involve "prompt size" as a concept, the paper does not measure the trade-off between prompt length and code quality, nor does it study the code generation domain, failing to meet any of the inclusion criteria (a-f).
